from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """Convert a list of airport‑related triples into a readable sentence."""
    if not triples:
        return ""
    # Simple template mapping for common predicates
    # Full‑sentence templates for the first (main) clause
    main_templates = {
        "cityServed": "{subject} serves {object}",
        "location": "{subject} is located in {object}",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevel": "{subject} lies {object} meters above sea level",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInFeet": "{subject} lies {object} feet above sea level",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInMetres": "{subject} lies {object} metres above sea level",
        "operatingOrganisation": "{subject} is operated by {object}",
        "runwayLength": "{subject} has a runway length of {object} metres",
        "runwayName": "{subject} has runway {object}",
        "country": "{subject} is in {object}",
        "isPartOf": "{subject} is part of {object}",
        "1stRunwayLengthFeet": "{subject} has a first runway {object} feet long",
        "1stRunwayLengthMetre": "{subject} has a first runway {object} metres long",
        "1stRunwayNumber": "{subject} has runway number {object}",
        "runwaySurfaceType": "{subject} runway surface is {object}",
    }
    # Short‑phrase templates for subsequent clauses (omit repeated subject)
    suffix_templates = {
        "cityServed": "serves {object}",
        "location": "located in {object}",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevel": "lying {object} meters above sea level",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInFeet": "lying {object} feet above sea level",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInMetres": "lying {object} metres above sea level",
        "operatingOrganisation": "operated by {object}",
        "runwayLength": "has a runway length of {object} metres",
        "runwayName": "has runway {object}",
        "country": "is in {object}",
        "isPartOf": "is part of {object}",
        "1stRunwayLengthFeet": "has a first runway {object} feet long",
        "1stRunwayLengthMetre": "has a first runway {object} metres long",
        "1stRunwayNumber": "has runway number {object}",
        "runwaySurfaceType": "runway surface is {object}",
    }
    # Expanded clause templates for richer language
    clause_templates = {
        "cityServed": "serves {object}",
        "location": "is located in {object}",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevel": "lies {object} metres above sea level",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInFeet": "lies {object} feet above sea level",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInMetres": "lies {object} metres above sea level",
        "operatingOrganisation": "is operated by {object}",
        "runwayLength": "has a runway length of {object} metres",
        "runwayName": "has runway {object}",
        "country": "is in {object}",
        "isPartOf": "is part of {object}",
        "1stRunwayLengthFeet": "has a first runway {object} feet long",
        "1stRunwayLengthMetre": "has a first runway {object} metres long",
        "1stRunwayNumber": "has runway number {object}",
        "runwaySurfaceType": "runway surface is {object}",
        "battle": "participated in {object}",
        "aircraftFighter": "operates fighter aircraft {object}",
        "aircraftHelicopter": "operates helicopter {object}",
        "transportAircraft": "operates transport aircraft {object}",
        "attackAircraft": "operates attack aircraft {object}",
        "regionServed": "serves the region {object}",
        "hubAirport": "has hub airport {object}",
        "owner": "is owned by {object}",
        "leader": "has leader {object}",
        "capital": "has capital {object}",
        "language": "uses language {object}",
        "officialLanguage": "has official language {object}",
        "currency": "uses currency {object}",
        "postalCode": "has postal code {object}",
        "areaCode": "has area code {object}",
        "foundingYear": "was founded in {object}",
        "chief": "has chief {object}",
        # fallback for any other predicate
    }

    clauses = []
    previous_subject = None
    for idx, t in enumerate(triples):
        # Determine whether we need to repeat the subject
        repeat_subject = (t.subject != previous_subject)
        previous_subject = t.subject

        if idx == 0 or repeat_subject:
            # First clause or subject change – try to use a template that includes the subject
            tmpl = main_templates.get(t.predicate) or clause_templates.get(t.predicate)
            if tmpl:
                if "{subject}" in tmpl:
                    clause = tmpl.format(subject=t.subject, object=t.object)
                else:
                    # Template does not contain a subject placeholder (e.g., "has leader {object}")
                    clause = f"{t.subject} " + tmpl.format(object=t.object)
            else:
                clause = f"{t.subject} {t.predicate.replace('_', ' ')} {t.object}"
        else:
            # Subsequent clause with same subject – use suffix style (no subject)
            tmpl = clause_templates.get(t.predicate)
            if tmpl:
                clause = tmpl.format(object=t.object)
            else:
                clause = f"{t.predicate.replace('_', ' ')} {t.object}"
        clauses.append(clause)

    # Assemble a single fluent sentence
    if len(clauses) == 1:
        return clauses[0] + "."
    # join all but last with commas, add ', and' before the final clause
    return ", ".join(clauses[:-1]) + ", and " + clauses[-1] + "."

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
